cases.description: ISERR is TRUE for a #DIV/0! error (any error except #N/A)

Key distinguishing case: ISERR is documented to return FALSE specifically for #N/A, unlike ISERROR (TRUE) -- 'ISERR: Value refers to any error value except #N/A' per https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/is-functions-0f2d7971-6019-40a0-a171-f2d869135665
cases.expected_note: This is the whole point of ISERR existing separately from ISERROR: it deliberately excludes #N/A.

cases.description: ISERR is TRUE for a #VALUE! error (not #N/A, so it counts)
